old_claim.note: Village Settlements provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The source-backed examples are permanent or semi-permanent Neolithic settlement houses, so village settlement context supplies the architectural demand for mud-brick construction.

source_shape.source_locator: Smarthistory describes Pre-Pottery Neolithic A Jericho as a large settlement with circular homes made with mud brick and domed roofs.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The mud-brick evidence is embedded in Neolithic settlement house construction at Jericho.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is contextual rather than material: the source shows mud bricks serving settled architecture, replacing an unsourced generic settlement claim.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: village_settlements remains an enabling prerequisite for the scoped Neolithic mud-brick architecture node.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to isolated loose bricks or craft production outside settlement architecture.
A stronger source shows early sun-dried mud bricks were first used outside permanent or semi-permanent buildings.
The village_settlements node is narrowed to a later agricultural-village definition that excludes PPNA Jericho.
short_reason: The mud-brick source is specifically Neolithic settlement house construction.
